qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Consider the following recursive C function that takes two arguments. unsigned int foo(unsigned int n, unsigned int r) { if (n>0) return ((n%r) + foo(n/r, r)); else return 0; } What is the return value of the function when it is called as A. B. C. D. ?

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Consider the following recursive C function that takes two arguments. unsigned int foo(unsigned int n, unsigned int r) { if (n>0) return ((n%r) + foo(n/r, r)); else return 0; } What is the return value of the function when it is called as A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Consider the following function: int unknown(int n){ int i, j, k=0; for (i=n/2; i<=n; i++) for (j=2; j<=n; j=j*2) k = k + n/2; return (k); }

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Consider the following C function in which size is the number of elements in the array E: int MyX(int *E, unsigned int size)
